summary: Opus 3-tier catalyst sweep (chunk 14 (OLPX-PLTR)): scan -> deep -> skeptic

survivors_flagged.one_line: Definitive Henkel merger at $2.06 cash, 75% written consent delivered Mar-26, DEFM14C filed, awaiting regulatory close 2026; but $2.04 px = ~1% gross spread, asymmetry largely priced.

Definitive ~$130M BNCCORP/BNC Bank acquisition + elimination of Up-C structure, targeted Q4-2026 close pending OCC/Fed/FDIC + BNCC vote; real dated structural-simplification catalyst (BNCC is the cleaner arb leg).
Strategic-review Phase 2 (external/portfolio) concluding end-Q2 2026 — ~3wks out — alongside active $500M buyback + serial dividend raises; dated value-creation trigger.
CPUC vote on $7.5B wildfire-cost securitization to retire ~$6B debt + dividend-doubling ramp — real dated deleveraging trigger, but leans utility re-rate over pure special-sit.
Dated activist+structural: board-declassification proposal (UNITE HERE) backed by ISS & Glass Lewis at June 16 2026 AGM, plus live M&A-target narrative after Caesars/Fertitta take-private.

Dated clinical binary: deucrictibant CHAPTER-3 prophylaxis topline Q3 2026 + NDA (IR on-demand) filing 1H26; RAPIDe-3 pivotal already positive (re-rated). One real forward readout, asymmetry not clearly mispriced.
Elliott $1B convert (3/3, fired) but activist board seat + ~$2B total buyback targeted to complete by mid-2026 = dated capital-return/structural; modest residual asymmetry.
LIVE special-sit: Special Committee strategic-alternatives review announced 4/6/2026 w/ Morgan Stanley incl. possible sale; controlled co (Giant/Yuzhu Shi). One real dated process, outcome uncertain.

verified.catalyst: Definitive all-cash acquisition by Henkel at $2.06/share ($1.4B), stockholder consent (75%, Advent) already delivered, awaiting antitrust/FDI clearances in US/Germany/Australia/UK before close.
verified.dated_milestone: Regulatory clearances pending — German antitrust filing made May 15, 2026; expected close H2 2026; outside date Mar 31, 2027 (extendable to Sep 30, 2027).
verified.instrument: stock (cash merger; no equity-upside optionality at $2.06 fixed price)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (Henkel named counterparty, definitive merger agreement) + G2 (concrete $2.06 all-cash commitment, signed, 75% written consent secured). G3 fails — no currently-unpriced figure; the ~1% spread IS the consensus deal-completion discount.
verified.edge: L — at $2.04 vs $2.06 the gross spread is ~1% (~1-4% annualized over a 3-12mo close), fully priced near the year-high; ~$0.02 upside vs ~$1.00 downside on a break (standalone ~52wk-low ~$1.00), so asymmetry is unfavorable and there is no unpriced catalyst leg.
verified.primary_source: OLPX DEFM14C (SEC EDGAR, filed ~Apr 2026) + MLex "Henkel filed Olaplex acquisition for German approval on May 15" (May 2026); FMP quote $2.04 (Jun 6, 2026).
verified.analysis: The merger is real, named, and committed: Henkel will pay $2.06/share cash, the controlling Advent stake (75%) delivered written consent Mar 26, 2026, and the DEFM14C is filed, so the only open conditions are antitrust/FDI clearances (German filing made May 15, 2026; HSR/Australia/UK pending). However the stock at $2.04 is already pinned to deal value at its year-high, leaving a ~1% gross spread that fully reflects near-certain close — the catalyst has effectively re-rated the name and the remaining payoff is a small, asymmetric carry trade, not actionable alpha. Clean enough to keep on WATCH for a closing-confirmation 8-K, but it does not clear the unpriced-edge bar for ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: OppFi's definitive ~$130M acquisition of BNCCORP/BNC National Bank (target gets $19.375 cash + 1.90 OPFI shares) converts OPFI into a bank holding company, alongside the already-completed Up-C collapse + TRA termination.
verified.dated_milestone: Targeted Q4-2026 close pending OCC/Fed/FDIC approvals + BNCC shareholder vote; next gate = S-4/proxy filing (not yet filed as of the May 7, 2026 Form 425) then BNCC special meeting.
verified.instrument: equity (OPFI common); arb leg is BNCC, not OPFI
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: BNCCORP/BNC National Bank, definitive merger agreement) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed deal, fixed $19.375 cash + 1.90-share exchange ratio, ~$130M)
verified.edge: L — on OPFI you hold the ACQUIRER, not the target, so no merger-arb premium accrues here; deal terms have been public and largely re-rated since the Apr 28-29 announcement, the Up-C/TRA simplification already FIRED, and OPFI carries dilution (BNCC holders get ~7% + 1.90 shares each), $40.8M TRA cash outflow, integration and BHC-conversion regulatory risk. The only residual spread (~3% gross, $35.16 deal value vs $34.08 BNCC) sits on the BNCC leg, which trades OTC at ~205 shares/day and is effectively uninvestable, plus the stock-heavy consideration means it is not a clean cash arb.
verified.primary_source: OppFi 8-K / PR & Form 425, SEC EDGAR (CIK 0001818502), announced Apr 29, 2026; Form 425 dated May 7, 2026; current quotes BNCC $34.08 / OPFI $8.31 (FMP, 2026-06-06).
verified.analysis: The catalyst is genuine, specific and dated (definitive agreement signed Apr 28-29 2026, ~$130M, Q4-2026 targeted close, OCC/Fed/FDIC + BNCC vote pending, S-4 still to be filed), satisfying G1/G2 — so it is not NONE. But the edge is mis-located: this is scored on OPFI, the acquirer, where there is no arb premium and the terms have been public/re-rated for ~5 weeks; the triage note itself concedes BNCC is the cleaner arb leg, and that leg is an illiquid OTC name (205 shares/day) with stock-heavy, non-cash consideration. The structural-simplification half (Up-C collapse + TRA termination) already FIRED on Apr 28, so what remains is a low-edge, dilution-and-regulatory-risk-bearing acquirer position rather than a capturable unpriced forward catalyst on OPFI.

verified.catalyst: Pitney Bowes plans to commence the external "Phase 2" of its strategic review by end-Q2 2026, retaining independent financial/legal advisors to evaluate all alternatives to its standalone trajectory (potential portfolio divestiture, e.g. global e-commerce), atop an ongoing $500M buyback and serial dividend raises.
verified.dated_milestone: Initiate external/Phase 2 strategic review by end of Q2 2026 (~June 30, 2026), ~3 weeks out
verified.bloom_gates: None pass strictly: G1 fails (no named counterparty/acquirer; advisors only being "evaluated/interviewed", none retained), G2 weak/fail (commitment is merely to begin a review of alternatives, not a binding transaction or structural action), G3 fails (no segment sum-of-parts or divestiture-proceeds figure disclosed).
verified.edge: L — public, repeated mgmt guidance since Feb 2026; Hold consensus, targets clustered $14.88-$16.74 at/below ~$16.72 spot, no embedded breakup premium and no informational edge; soft process milestone, not a discrete value event.
verified.primary_source: Pitney Bowes Q1 2026 CEO letter / 8-K, SEC EDGAR (filing dated Feb 17, 2026; reaffirmed Q1 2026 earnings, early May 2026): "still on track to commence our strategic review's second phase by the end of the second quarter ... working with independent financial and legal advisors to review all potential alternatives to our standalone value creation trajectory."
verified.analysis: The catalyst is genuine, idiosyncratic and dated (~3wks out), but it is a textbook soft strategic-review process step: the company commits only to "commence Phase 2" / "initiate the external portion" and retain advisors to "review all potential alternatives" — there is no named counterparty (G1), no concrete committed transaction (G2), and no specific unpriced figure (G3), so it fails the strict gate. End-Q2 likely yields another process update (advisor appointments / scope), not a crystallizing divestiture or sale on a fixed date, and the ongoing $500M buyback + dividend raises are continuous capital-return programs already in motion rather than forward triggers. Real and worth monitoring for an actual transaction out of the external phase, hence WATCH at score 4, below the flagged 6.

verified.catalyst: Flagged $7.5B wildfire-cost securitization + dividend-doubling, but both are already-fired (2021 stress-test recovery bonds; dividend already doubled to $0.20 annual run-rate), leaving only a routine Q2-2026 Kincade/Dixie AB 1054 cost-recovery proposed decision.
verified.dated_milestone: Kincade & Dixie AB 1054 wildfire cost-recovery proposed decision targeted Q2 2026 (no exact CPUC voting date surfaced); next dividend ex/record 2026-06-30, pay 2026-07-15.
verified.bloom_gates: none (no forward G1 named counterparty / G2 concrete commitment / G3 specific currently-unpriced figure — the cited securitization+dividend triggers already fired; Kincade/Dixie recovery is a routine regulated AB 1054 mechanic, not unpriced)
verified.edge: L — large-cap S&P utility (~$30B+) saturated with sell-side coverage; deleveraging glide path and SB 254/securitization fully telegraphed in the 2026–2030 financing plan and 2026 proxy, so no informational edge.
verified.primary_source: FMP PCG news stream (search-stock-news, 2026-05-01 to 2026-06-05) shows no securitization vote; Utility Dive 2021 securitization coverage; PG&E Q1-2026 8-K earnings presentation / 2026 DEF 14A proxy ($73B 2026–2030 plan, SB 254 securitization, dividend-to-20%-by-2028); PG&E Kincade/Dixie AB 1054 cost-recovery FAQ (proposed decision targeted Q2 2026).
verified.analysis: The flagged thesis conflates already-fired events: the $7.5B recovery-bond securitization to retire ~$6B debt was authorized in 2021 post-bankruptcy, and the dividend doubling to $0.20 annual is the current declared run-rate (Q2-2026 dividend $0.05/qtr), with the 20%-payout-by-2028 path a multi-year glide, not a dated trigger. No June-2026 CPUC securitization vote exists — May-1-to-Jun-5 news is value-stock filler, a solar milestone, and wildfire ops. The only genuinely forward item, the Q2-2026 Kincade/Dixie AB 1054 cost-recovery proposed decision (CPUC review of ~$674M Wildfire-Fund costs through 9/30/25 plus rate recovery), is a generic regulated-utility re-rate without a specific unpriced figure or dated voting day — failing the strict real+specific+forward+not-macro gate; the triage's own "leans utility re-rate" caveat is correct.

verified.catalyst: UNITE HERE's non-binding board-declassification proposal goes to a shareholder vote at PENN's June 16, 2026 AGM with ISS + Glass Lewis backing, against a backdrop of regional-casino consolidation chatter after the Caesars/Fertitta take-private.
verified.dated_milestone: June 16, 2026 PENN Annual Meeting (declassification vote); separately Caesars/Fertitta go-shop ends July 11, 2026
verified.bloom_gates: none (G1 no named bidder for PENN; G2 precatory governance item, no concrete capital/asset commitment; G3 no specific unpriced figure)
verified.edge: L — labor-union precatory proposal that even if passed only changes election cadence (and PENN ignored an identical 2010-approved measure); M&A angle is undated sector-consolidation speculation already largely re-rated.
verified.primary_source: BusinessWire, "ISS and Glass Lewis Recommend FOR Board Declassification Proposal at PENN Entertainment," June 3, 2026; corroborated by casino.org (Jun 2026) and Caesars 8-K/Seeking Alpha on Fertitta $31/sh take-private with go-shop through July 11, 2026.
verified.analysis: The declassification vote is genuinely real, dated (June 16, 2026) and forward, with ISS and Glass Lewis both recommending FOR — but it is a non-binding UNITE HERE labor governance proposal that unlocks no value on a known timeline (the board already ignored a shareholder-approved 2010 version), so it fails all three Bloom gates. The "M&A-target" thesis has no named bidder, no offer, and no dated milestone for PENN — it is a Jefferies analyst tagging PENN as one of several medium-term regional-consolidation candidates after the Caesars/Fertitta deal, i.e. a sector/macro bet the gate excludes. With PENN at $19.23 vs a $11.65 52-wk low and $16.41 50-day, near its $20.61 high, the consolidation narrative is already substantially priced in.

verified.catalyst: CHAPTER-3 Phase 3 topline (deucrictibant 40mg ER for HAE prophylaxis, ~81 pts, 24wk) is the one genuine forward readout, with the on-demand NDA filing a near-certain regulatory follow-through on already-positive RAPIDe-3.
verified.dated_milestone: CHAPTER-3 prophylaxis topline expected 3Q2026; on-demand NDA filing 1H2026
verified.instrument: equity (HAE binary; OTM calls dated past 3Q2026 if playing the readout, but asymmetry thin)
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (partial) — a specific dated forward event (CHAPTER-3 topline 3Q2026), but NOT a clearly currently-unpriced figure; no named counterparty (G1) or new concrete commitment (G2) beyond on-track guidance.
verified.edge: L — mechanism already de-risked by CHAPTER-1 Phase 2 (84.5% attack-rate cut at the identical 40mg commercial dose, p=0.0008) and franchise re-rated on positive RAPIDe-3; CHAPTER-3 is a high-prior confirmatory Phase 3, not a coin-flip, so asymmetry is not clearly mispriced.
verified.primary_source: Pharvaris "Outlines 2026 Strategic Priorities," GlobeNewswire, 2026-01-12 (CHAPTER-3 3Q2026 topline, NDA 1H2026, runway into 1H2027); RAPIDe-3 positive topline PR + CHAPTER-1 Phase 2 results PR (84.5% reduction); FMP quote 2026-06-06 ($31.09).
verified.analysis: PHVS has exactly one real dated forward catalyst — CHAPTER-3 prophylaxis Phase 3 topline in 3Q2026 — plus a 1H2026 on-demand NDA that is regulatory follow-through, not a binary. The asymmetry is weak: the B2-antagonist prophylaxis mechanism is already heavily de-risked by the completed CHAPTER-1 Phase 2 (84.5% monthly-attack-rate reduction at the same 40mg dose) and the franchise has already re-rated on positive RAPIDe-3 (primary met, p<0.0001), with the stock at $31 near its 52wk high of $33 vs a $16 low. Confirms the triage read — genuine forward readout but not clearly mispriced; WATCH for the 3Q2026 print rather than an active dated-binary entry.

verified.catalyst: Elliott's $1B 1.75% convertible (conv. ~$22.72) plus a board seat and ~$2B near-term buyback announced Mar 3, 2026 — a capital-return/governance event that is now fired and the dated near-term piece executed.
verified.dated_milestone: ASR scheduled to complete no later than May 1, 2026 (settled May 2026); ~$2B aggregate H1-2026 buyback confirmed "completed" at Q1-2026 earnings — both dates now PAST. Next: no dated milestone on the ~$2B residual $3.5B authorization (no time limit, management discretion).
verified.instrument: equity (residual buyback only); convertible already placed with Elliott — not retail-accessible
verified.bloom_gates: None pass forward. G1: counterparty (Elliott/Goldman ASR) named but its commitment already executed, not forward. G2: the concrete near-term commitment ($1B ASR + $500M 10b5-1) is done; remaining $3.5B authorization is open-ended/discretionary, no dated commitment. G3: $1B convert + $2B near-term buyback are public and re-rated, not an unpriced figure.
verified.edge: L — the asymmetry was the Mar 3 announcement (stock popped ~9% then faded); price $21.42 now sits BELOW the $22.72 conversion and below 200d avg $25.45, i.e. the catalyst is priced/faded, not anticipatory.
verified.primary_source: Pinterest 8-K / press release "Pinterest Announces $1 Billion Strategic Investment from Elliott and $2 Billion of Near-Term Share Repurchases" (Mar 3, 2026) + Q1-2026 earnings release (reported late Apr 2026, "~$2 billion of share repurchases completed"); FMP quote $21.42 (Jun 6, 2026).
verified.analysis: This is a dated capital-return/governance event that has already fired: the $1B Elliott convertible was placed Mar 3, 2026, the $1B ASR was scheduled to settle no later than May 1, 2026, and Pinterest confirmed ~$2B of near-term repurchases "completed" at Q1-2026 earnings. The only forward element is ~$2B remaining on the $3.5B authorization, but it carries no time limit and is at management discretion — failing the DATED + FORWARD test and Bloom G1/G2/G3 for a fresh unpriced catalyst. Residual asymmetry is modest and now more an ad-demand/fundamental story than a discrete dated catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Special Committee strategic-alternatives review (Morgan Stanley advisor) announced 4/6/2026 — exploratory, no named buyer, no offer, company won't disclose until/unless a deal is approved.
verified.dated_milestone: No dated forward milestone — company explicitly will not disclose developments until the Board approves a course of action; open-ended, no deadline/vote scheduled.
verified.instrument: equity (PLTK common); controlled co (Giant/Yuzhu Shi majority) caps minority optionality
verified.bloom_gates: None pass. G1 (named counterparty): FAIL — only Morgan Stanley as advisor, no bidder. G2 (concrete commitment): FAIL — explicit "no assurance of any transaction." G3 (specific unpriced figure): FAIL — no offer price/valuation disclosed.
verified.edge: L — pop already fired and fully faded; open-ended process with no named buyer/price/date and a controlled-company structure leaves no datable, unpriced forward edge.
verified.primary_source: Playtika 8-K / GlobeNewswire press release "Playtika Announces Review of Strategic Alternatives" (2026-04-06); confirmed via SEC EDGAR strategicreviewprocesspres.htm and FMP quote PLTK $3.09 (2026-06-05 close).
verified.analysis: The 4/6/2026 review is real and dated with a named advisor (Morgan Stanley) but fails the strict gate: no named counterparty, no concrete commitment ("no assurance"), no disclosed price, and the company explicitly committed NOT to disclose developments until a deal is approved — so there is no dated forward milestone. The +16.79% announcement pop has fully reversed (now $3.09, below pre-announcement base and near the $2.64 52-wk low; under 50d/200d MAs), and May–June shareholder-fraud law-firm investigations add overhang. Catalyst is fired and re-rating negatively, not forward — tier NONE.
